texto: Perhaps the breaking of the human spirit into submissive, thoughtless robots is the most terrible feature of Stalin’s Russia. Humanity is bowed down. Every one cringes before his superiors, and those who abase themselves seek outlets in bullying and terrifying the unfortunates beneath them. Integrity, courage and charity disappear in the stifling atmosphere of cant, falsehood and terror.
explicacao: Citação de Freda Utley sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Perhaps the breaking of the human spirit into submissive, thoughtless robots is the most terrible…” articula fe, communism, marxism em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como marxism e russia.

frase_original: Perhaps the breaking of the human spirit into submissive, thoughtless robots is the most terrible feature of Stalin’s Russia. Humanity is bowed down. Every one cringes before his superiors, and those who abase themselves seek outlets in bullying and terrifying the unfortunates beneath them. Integrity, courage and charity disappear in the stifling atmosphere of cant, falsehood and terror.

texto: Why is it that we who have enjoyed the human free­doms which our forefathers fought so hard to win and to bequeath to us, do not, with the example of Russia before us, realize the horrors of life without freedom? Why is it that we cannot understand that there is no such thing as embracing Communism as an experiment? It is a one-way street, ending in a cul de sac of secret police terror, firing squads for the intellectuals and leaders and concentration camps and slave labor for the masses. There is no turning back; there is no escape.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Freda Utley que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Why is it that we who have enjoyed the human free­doms which our forefathers fought so hard to wi…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre lideranca, bolshevism, communism, com foco em termino e estudos.
